commentary_text: The sportsbook consensus assigns Bosnia and Herzegovina a 68% win probability versus the model's 47%—a 21-percentage-point gap favoring the home-side favorite. The market also prices Qatar significantly lower at 13% compared to the model's 23%. This sharpening of conviction toward Bosnia and Herzegovina could reflect recent lineup changes, injury developments, or current form shifts that the model's Elo-based framework has not yet incorporated. The dashboard presents this divergence as a signal for review; neither the statistical model nor the aggregate market has demonstrated reliable superiority on such matches.
